title: Technology Mediation and the Good Life: A Postphenomenological Analysis
abstract: This article develops a postphenomenological framework for understanding how technologies mediate moral experience. Drawing on Verbeek and Ihde, we argue that technology mediation theory must be extended to account for emerging AI systems.

title: Algorithmic Fairness in Hiring: An Empirical Study of Bias Detection Tools
abstract: We conduct a comparative analysis of five algorithmic fairness tools applied to hiring datasets. Our survey of 200 HR professionals reveals significant gaps between technical fairness metrics and organizational justice perceptions.

title: Actor-Network Theory Meets Autonomous Vehicles: Reassembling the Social in Self-Driving
abstract: Applying Latourian actor-network theory to the autonomous vehicle ecosystem, this article traces how human and non-human actors co-construct the sociotechnical assemblage of self-driving. We present ethnographic observations from three AV testing sites.

title: The Concept of Digital Sovereignty: A Genealogical Investigation
abstract: This paper reconstructs the genealogy of digital sovereignty from Westphalian sovereignty through cyber-sovereignty to contemporary data governance debates. We argue that the concept requires fundamental rethinking in light of distributed architectures.

title: Heidegger's Question Concerning AI: Gestell Revisited
abstract: We revisit Heidegger's concept of Gestell (enframing) in relation to artificial intelligence systems. While acknowledging the interpretive challenges of applying Heidegger to contemporary technology, we propose that the standing-reserve framework illuminates aspects of AI that instrumental analyses miss.

title: Responsible Innovation in Practice: A Mixed-Methods Study of Three European Living Labs
abstract: This article presents findings from a mixed-methods investigation of responsible innovation frameworks deployed in three European living labs. We combine qualitative interviews (n=45) with survey data (n=312) to evaluate whether RI principles translate into measurable outcomes.
